title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 305.130 - Unlawful to operate without an airman certificate and certificate of airworthiness
text:
305.130.  Unlawful to operate without an airman certificate and certificate of airworthiness. — It shall be unlawful for any person to operate any aircraft within this state in carrying a passenger or passengers, or any property, or in the prosecution of a business or commercial enterprise, or for instruction in the art of flying, without an* airman certificate for such purposes issued by the Federal Aviation Administration, and without a valid certificate of airworthiness for such aircraft issued by the Federal Aviation Administration.
(RSMo 1939 § 15118, A.L. 2002 S.B. 701) 
Prior revision: 1929 § 13917 
*Word "a" appears in original rolls.
